title: Simon Douville
content:
People.ContactEmail sdouville@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Montréal
Simon Douville is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault's National Tax Group and one of Canada's leading commodity tax practitioners. He advises sophisticated Canadian and non-resident clients on all aspects of commodity taxation, including the Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST), the Quebec sales tax, provincial sales taxes, excise taxes, fuel taxes, insurance premium taxes, and land transfer taxes.
Known for his ability to pair deep technical expertise with creative, commercially-grounded problem solving, Simon delivers solutions that are practical, precisely tailored, and built around each client's specific objectives. He has significant experience advising clients on corporate reorganizations, mergers and acquisitions, and cross-border transactions.
Simon has built a nationally recognized practice advising some of the largest taxpayers in the financial services industry, including banks, insurance companies (including segregated funds), pension plans, mutual funds and other investment plans as well as fund managers on all commodity tax aspects relating to their business, including on their status as financial institutions for GST/HST purposes, their self-assessment obligations, and their entitlement to claim input tax credits. He frequently assists clients in this space to develop tax-efficient structures in respect of mergers, acquisitions, corporate reorganizations and cross-border transactions.
Simon regularly handles complex audits for large public and private companies across a broad range of industries, including financial services, telecommunications, real estate, energy, oil and gas, and retail. Simon's extensive technical expertise in the field of commodity taxation has led him to represent clients before the Tax Court of Canada, the Federal Court of Appeal as well as provincial trial and appellate courts.
Simon's recent and ongoing representations include:
- President's Choice Bank in an appeal regarding their entitlement to claim notional input tax credits;
- Glencore in an appeal regarding whether GST/HST can be set-off as between commercial parties;
- Rogers in a trial regarding the availability of tax credits relating to its goodwill credits;
- CIBC in an appeal regarding its entitlement to claim input tax credits in connection with foreign interchange services.
Simon's clients speak to both his technical depth and his practical judgment:

#### Thought Leadership and Teaching
Simon is a member of the invitation-only GST Leaders Forum. He received the 2021 Norman Guerin Award for his outstanding contribution to the advancement of commodity tax studies for his paper "Claim me if you can — Financial institutions and ITCs".
Simon teaches the commodity tax course at the Université du Québec à Montréal (UQAM) and is a frequent speaker on commodity tax issues at leading industry conferences, including the Tax Executives Institute, the Commodity Tax Symposium, the Canadian Life and Health Insurance Industry Association (CLHIA), and the Pension Investment Association of Canada (PIAC).

#### Education and Bar Admissions
Simon holds an LLM in Tax Law (HEC Montréal) and an LLB (Université de Montréal). He is a member of the Barreau du Québec and the Law Society of Ontario.
- The Canadian Legal Lexpert Directory Leading Lawyer: Commodity Tax/Customs
